message:
feat(policy): tick 152 inject_with_cx routes helper calls through cx-first siblings (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Third post-storage tick. Closes the 2 remaining gaps in policy.rs
cx-first coverage (was 7/9, now 9/9) and migrates the legacy calls
inside `inject_with_cx` onto the new siblings so the fn is now
cancellation-interruptible at every async seam it owns.

New siblings:
- maybe_inject_trauma_feedback_with_cx — routes through
  `send_text_with_options_with_cx` so synthetic trauma-feedback
  injections bail when the parent cx is cancelled. The legacy
  method has a tracing::warn fallback; the cx-first sibling
  mirrors that with a "(cx path)" suffix so log grep can
  distinguish the path that produced the error.
- find_workflow_start_action_id_with_cx — routes through
  `get_audit_actions_with_cx`. Returns `None` on cancellation to
  match the legacy "swallow errors → None" contract, so the
  decision-context capture degrades gracefully rather than
  failing the parent inject.

Call-site migrations in `inject_with_cx` (6376+):
- Line 6469 (was `maybe_inject_trauma_feedback`) →
  `maybe_inject_trauma_feedback_with_cx(cx, ...)`
- Line 6522 (was `find_workflow_start_action_id`) →
  `find_workflow_start_action_id_with_cx(cx, ...)`

Legacy `inject` preserved unchanged; its two call sites (6937 and
6991) still route through the ambient legacy helpers. Byte parity
preserved — legacy tests still pass without modification.

Verified via fork bypass: 456/456 policy:: + tantivy_policy:: tests
pass.

policy.rs cx-first coverage: 9/9 async fns (was 7/9).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
